task_completion.description: Agent logged into the Magento Admin panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Magento Admin dashboard or catalog page is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the edit page for 'Business Laptop Pro'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The product edit page is open, showing the product name 'Business Laptop Pro' and/or SKU 'LAPTOP-001' in the header or basic settings.

task_completion.description: Agent updated the URL Key and checked the permanent redirect option.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Search Engine Optimization' section, the 'URL Key' field contains exactly 'business-laptop-pro-2025' and the checkbox for 'Create Permanent Redirect for old URL' is checked.
In the 'Search Engine Optimization' section, the 'Meta Title' field contains exactly 'Business Laptop Pro 15-inch | Enterprise Edition'.

In the 'Search Engine Optimization' section, the 'Meta Description' field contains exactly 'Upgrade your workflow with the Business Laptop Pro. Featuring 16GB RAM, 512GB SSD, and all-day battery life. Free shipping on orders over $500.'.
A green success message stating 'You saved the product.' is visible at the top of the product page, or the save button shows a loading/saved state.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Magento Admin interface in a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a real web browser with the Magento Admin UI, not a text editor, mockup, or local HTML file.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing magento CLI commands, curl requests, or direct database queries being used to update the product.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely entered the SEO data into the web form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text inputs appear in the correct Magento form fields, rather than being edited into the DOM via developer tools.
